source_en: I am not such a fool but can appreciate it.
target_ja: その良さがわからぬばかではない。
group_key: i am not such a fool but can appreciate it.

source_en: I'm always confusing John with Paul.
target_ja: いつもジョンとポールが分からなくなっちゃうのよ。
group_key: i'm always confusing john with paul.

source_en: Down with the Government!
target_ja: 政府打倒。
group_key: down with the government!

source_en: I am becoming a working adult in January.
target_ja: 社会人になって初めてのお正月です。
group_key: i am becoming a working adult in january.

source_en: My phlegm has blood in it.
target_ja: 血たんが出ます。
group_key: my phlegm has blood in it.

source_en: Is Tom still in love with Mary?
target_ja: トムはまだメアリーとラブラブなの？
group_key: is tom still in love with mary?

source_en: Let me introduce you to him.
target_ja: 彼にあなたを紹介させて下さい。
group_key: let me introduce you to him.

source_en: May I sneak in? My flight is leaving soon.
target_ja: 通していただけますか。飛行機が出ちゃいますので。
group_key: may i sneak in? my flight is leaving soon.

source_en: I may have nothing now, but I shall always have something to remember, while you have no memories at all.
target_ja: わたしは今は何も持っていないかもしれない、わたしはいつも何か思い出があるが、あなたには少しも思い出がない。
group_key: i may have nothing now, but i shall always have something to remember, while you have no memories at all.

source_en: I shouldn't have sent that email.
target_ja: あの電子メールを送るんじゃなかった。
group_key: i shouldn't have sent that email.

source_en: Is that why you came here?
target_ja: それでここに来たの？
group_key: is that why you came here?

source_en: Here is the book you are looking for.
target_ja: 君の探している本だよ。
group_key: here is the book you are looking for.

source_en: We are doing business with many countries.
target_ja: 私どもは多くの国と商売をしています。
group_key: we are doing business with many countries.

source_en: There's a bank in front of the station.
target_ja: 駅前には銀行がある。
group_key: there's a bank in front of the station.

source_en: How it blows!
target_ja: なんと厳しい風だろう。
group_key: how it blows!

source_en: Do you think it'll rain today?
target_ja: 今日、雨が降ると思いますか。
group_key: do you think it'll rain today?

source_en: Few people, if any, could survive a nuclear war.
target_ja: 核戦争で生き残る人は、たとえいたとしてもほとんどいないだろう。
group_key: few people, if any, could survive a nuclear war.

source_en: I expect every day is chaos for families with small children.
target_ja: 小さな子供のいる家庭は、毎日がどたばたして大変だと思う。
group_key: i expect every day is chaos for families with small children.

source_en: Then you will have money and you will be happy.
target_ja: すると、あなたはお金をもらえてしあわせでしょう。
group_key: then you will have money and you will be happy.

source_en: He insulted me by calling me a donkey.
target_ja: 彼は私をロバと呼んで侮辱した。
group_key: he insulted me by calling me a donkey.

source_en: We had to push our way through the crowd.
target_ja: 混んでいたので人をかき分けて行かねばならなかった。
group_key: we had to push our way through the crowd.

source_en: It's for my family.
target_ja: それは私の家族へのものです。
group_key: it's for my family.

source_en: Turning to the left, you will find the gas station.
target_ja: 左に曲がるとガソリンスタンドがある。
group_key: turning to the left, you will find the gas station.

source_en: Do I need permission?
target_ja: 許可が必要ですか？
group_key: do i need permission?

source_en: He can cook as well as his wife.
target_ja: 彼は奥さんに劣らず料理がうまい。
group_key: he can cook as well as his wife.

source_en: My parents never allowed me to swim alone in the sea.
target_ja: 私の両親は決して私を一人では海で泳がせなかった。
group_key: my parents never allowed me to swim alone in the sea.

source_en: I needn't have hurried.
target_ja: 私は、急ぐ必要はなかったのに。
group_key: i needn't have hurried.

source_en: You seem to have mistaken me for my elder brother.
target_ja: あなたは私を兄と間違えておられたようですね。
group_key: you seem to have mistaken me for my elder brother.

source_en: We expect a very cold winter this year.
target_ja: 今年の冬は非常に寒くなることが予測されます。
group_key: we expect a very cold winter this year.

source_en: He has a lot of money.
target_ja: 彼は金持ちだ。
group_key: he has a lot of money.

source_en: That was the only way we could defend ourselves against all this terrible shooting.
target_ja: それだけが、この恐ろしい撃ち合いから身を守ることのできる唯一の方法だったの。
group_key: that was the only way we could defend ourselves against all this terrible shooting.

source_en: I have never been to that town.
target_ja: 私はその町へ行ったことがない。
group_key: i have never been to that town.

source_en: I was aware of that fact.
target_ja: 私はその事実を知っていましたよ。
group_key: i was aware of that fact.

source_en: Why isn't Tom here at this critical moment?
target_ja: なんでこの肝心な時にトムがいないんだよ！
group_key: why isn't tom here at this critical moment?

source_en: Some stores discount the price.
target_ja: 価格を値引きする店もあります。

source_en: The theme park was closed down last month.
target_ja: テーマパークは先月閉鎖されました。
group_key: the theme park was closed down last month.

source_en: I can't stand him.
target_ja: 僕は、あいつには我慢できない。
group_key: i can't stand him.

source_en: Last year in the Philippines, earthquakes and tidal waves resulted in the deaths of more than 6000 people.
target_ja: 昨年フィリッピンでは地震や津波のために六千人以上の死者があった。
group_key: last year in the philippines, earthquakes and tidal waves resulted in the deaths of more than 6000 people.

source_en: I'll ask how to get there.
target_ja: そこへ行くにはどうすればよいかを聞いてみましょう。
group_key: i'll ask how to get there.

source_en: Is Tatoeba like Facebook?
target_ja: タトエバというのはフェイスブックのようなものですか？
group_key: is tatoeba like facebook?

source_en: I'm worried that Tom will get hurt.
target_ja: トムが怪我をしないか心配なんだよ。

source_en: Write your name at the bottom of the paper.
target_ja: 用紙の下部に名前を書きなさい。
group_key: write your name at the bottom of the paper.

source_en: He is sometimes absent from school.
target_ja: 彼は時々学校を休みます。
group_key: he is sometimes absent from school.

source_en: About 50 percent of the firms in Japan have acknowledged the necessity of giving their workers longer holidays, and think summer holidays are needed to give their workers both mental and physical refreshment.
target_ja: 日本にある会社の約５０パーセントは、従業員にもっと長く休暇を与えることの必要性を認めており、精神的及び肉体的な休養を与えるためには夏休みが必要であると考えている。
group_key: about 50 percent of the firms in japan have acknowledged the necessity of giving their workers longer holidays, and think summer holidays are needed to give their workers both mental and physical refreshment.

source_en: The police were heading for a shake down.
target_ja: 警察は徹底的捜査をしようとしてた。
group_key: the police were heading for a shake down.

source_en: When was the car washed by Ken?
target_ja: その車はいつ健に洗われたのですか。
group_key: when was the car washed by ken?

source_en: My uncle blew in from Kobe.
target_ja: おじが神戸よりひょっこりやってきた。

source_en: The time has come when we must part.
target_ja: 別れる時がやって来た。

source_en: The next meeting will be on the twentieth of October.
target_ja: 次の会議は10月の20日です。

source_en: Are there a lot of guns?
target_ja: 銃はたくさんありますか。

source_en: Unfortunately it will be impossible for us to accept your proposal.
target_ja: 残念ながら、ご提案をお受けすることはできません。
group_key: unfortunately it will be impossible for us to accept your proposal.

source_en: Tom can't draw as well as me.
target_ja: トムは私ほど絵がうまくない。

source_en: Nakamatsu smiled bitterly at his own stupid question.
target_ja: 中松は自分の頓馬な質問に苦笑いしました。
group_key: nakamatsu smiled bitterly at his own stupid question.

source_en: Let's forget about bad things! Well, at least until we're reminded of them.
target_ja: 嫌なことは忘れよう！蒸し返されるまでは。
group_key: let's forget about bad things! well, at least until we're reminded of them.

source_en: Close your eyes, and listen carefully.

target_ja: コーヒーをもう一杯飲みたいな。
group_key: i could really go for another cup of coffee.

target_ja: 母が病気だったので、私が母の世話をした。

target_ja: 彼女は僕を捨てた。

target_ja: そんな悪い子と友達になるな。

target_ja: 彼は今日は上機嫌だ。

target_ja: 突然彼女は甲高い声で叫び出した。

source_en: I can't afford such a good camera as yours.
target_ja: 私には君が持っているようないいカメラを買う余裕がない。

source_en: You dropped a bookmark.
target_ja: しおり、落としましたよ。

source_en: No student was able to answer the question.
target_ja: 生徒は誰もその質問に答えられませんでした。

target_ja: 一雨きそうだよ。傘を持ってった方がいいね。

source_en: It's too late for regrets.
target_ja: 後悔先に立たず。

target_ja: 私は１９３３年生まれです。

target_ja: あなたは咳がありますか。

source_en: To be accurate, he is to blame.

target_ja: ６時の飛行機なのです。

target_ja: 彼女は私が彼女のお金を盗んだという理由で私を訴えた。

source_en: I didn't know you could drive.

target_ja: エミは体の不自由な人に席を譲った。

source_en: Do you often hear from him?
target_ja: 彼からたびたび便りがありますか。

target_ja: 彼女は大人になったらテニス選手になりたい。

target_ja: 私は彼に煙草を吸うなと忠告した。

source_en: Please put these books into that box.
target_ja: この本はあの箱に入れてください。

source_en: He'll probably win in the next election.
target_ja: 次の選挙では彼が当選するだろう。

source_en: I think Tom would be reasonable.
target_ja: トムは合理的だと思います。

source_en: I have almost no information about the problem.
target_ja: その問題については、何の情報も持っていない。

source_en: Tokyo is larger than Yokohama.
target_ja: 東京は横浜より大きい。

source_en: Be punctual for appointments, otherwise you'll lose face.

group_key: be punctual for appointments, otherwise you'll lose face.

source_en: He was greatly praised, while I was as much scolded.
target_ja: 彼は非常に誉められたがしかるに私は非常にしかられた。
group_key: he was greatly praised, while i was as much scolded.

source_en: I am glad it was someone else who got it.
target_ja: 対岸の火事で良かった。

source_en: The baby has been crying for almost ten minutes.
target_ja: その赤ちゃんはもう１０分近くも泣きっぱなしだ。
group_key: the baby has been crying for almost ten minutes.

source_en: I'm fine.
target_ja: 平気だよ。

source_en: May I come to see you this afternoon?
target_ja: 今日の午後お伺いしてもよろしいですか。

source_en: Tom just wanted to show off.
target_ja: トムは目立ちたかっただけだよ。

target_ja: その家の周りは垣根がめぐらされている。

source_en: Nobody's taking a bath right now.
target_ja: お風呂空いてるよ。

target_ja: 僕たち、泥んこ遊びが好きなんだ。

target_ja: 今や兄は大学生になったから、おおいに読書をしなければなりません。

source_en: It is about time you went to school.
target_ja: そろそろ学校に行く時間です。

source_en: We've decided to start next Sunday.
target_ja: 我々は次の日曜日に出発することに決めた。

source_en: I had intended to attend the meeting.
target_ja: 会合に出席するつもりだったのだが。

source_en: I cannot hear such a story without weeping.
target_ja: 私はそのような話を聞くとすぐ泣ける。
group_key: i cannot hear such a story without weeping.

source_en: Take the child away into the forest; I never want to see her again. Kill her, and bring me back this handkerchief soaked in her blood as proof.
target_ja: 「あの子を、森の中につれていっておくれ。わたしは、もうあの子を、二どと見たくないんだから。だが、おまえはあの子をころして、そのしょうこに、あの子の血を、このハンケチにつけてこなければなりません。」
group_key: take the child away into the forest; i never want to see her again. kill her, and bring me back this handkerchief soaked in her blood as proof.

source_en: I'm sorry. Did I wake you up?
target_ja: ごめん、起こしちゃった？
group_key: i'm sorry. did i wake you up?

target_ja: 彼は私を、怠慢だと言って叱った。

source_en: The main islands of Japan are Hokkaido, Shikoku, Honshu and Kyushu.
target_ja: 日本の主要な島は、北海道、四国、本州、九州です。
group_key: the main islands of japan are hokkaido, shikoku, honshu and kyushu.

source_en: When should we do that?
target_ja: それはいつやる？
group_key: when should we do that?

source_en: He was crowned with eternal victory.
target_ja: 彼は不滅の栄冠を得た。
group_key: he was crowned with eternal victory.

source_en: No one knows the fact.
target_ja: 誰もその事実を知りません。

source_en: He turned to a tall woman nearby.
target_ja: 彼の近くにいる背の高い婦人の方を振り向きました。
group_key: he turned to a tall woman nearby.

source_en: Men usually like wrestling as women do not.
target_ja: 女性はレスリングは好まないが男性はふつうに好きだ。

source_en: This bird can imitate the human voice.
target_ja: この鳥は人の声を真似できるんだ。
group_key: this bird can imitate the human voice.

source_en: The boy is tall for his age.
target_ja: その少年は年の割に背が高い。
group_key: the boy is tall for his age.

source_en: She tried to conceal the fact.
target_ja: 彼女は事実を隠そうとした。
group_key: she tried to conceal the fact.

source_en: Have you ever eaten insects?
target_ja: 虫って、食べたことある？
group_key: have you ever eaten insects?

source_en: Health is the first condition of happiness.
target_ja: 健康が幸福の第１条件です。

target_ja: トムは朝食前にシャワーを浴びた。

source_en: Since we can expect no help from others, let's try to do our best.
target_ja: 私達は他の人の援助を期待できないので全力を尽くすようにしよう。

target_ja: 私は無理に白状させられた。

source_en: It's a beautiful day.
target_ja: いい天気だなあ。
group_key: it's a beautiful day.

source_en: They discussed his proposals at the meeting.
target_ja: 会議で彼の提案が取り上げられた。

source_en: There's no chance that he'll recover.

source_en: Let me handle this.

source_en: Is there anything wrong about it?

source_en: You are a schoolteacher, aren't you?
target_ja: 学校の先生なんでしょ？
group_key: you are a schoolteacher, aren't you?

source_en: It is my practice to get up at 6 a.m.
target_ja: 朝６時に起きるのが私の習慣です。
group_key: it is my practice to get up at 6 a.m.

source_en: I have a lot more homework than Tom does.
target_ja: トムよりもっとたくさん宿題があるんだ。
group_key: i have a lot more homework than tom does.

source_en: Kate is looked up to by everybody.
target_ja: ケイトは皆に尊敬されている。
group_key: kate is looked up to by everybody.

source_en: This doesn't concern you, Tom.
target_ja: トムには関係ないんだ。
group_key: this doesn't concern you, tom.

source_en: My father is out in the garden now.
target_ja: 父は今、庭に出ています。
group_key: my father is out in the garden now.

source_en: This letter says that he will arrive on Monday.
target_ja: この手紙で彼は日曜日に着くといっている。
group_key: this letter says that he will arrive on monday.

source_en: I can't sell that to you.
target_ja: それをあなたに売ることはできません。
group_key: i can't sell that to you.

source_en: Refugees in that camp have been living from hand to mouth for a month.
target_ja: そのキャンプの難民は１ヶ月の間、その日暮らしの生活を送っている。
group_key: refugees in that camp have been living from hand to mouth for a month.

source_en: If you go around treating people poorly, it'll eventually bite you in the back.
target_ja: 人に意地悪すると、いつか自分に戻ってくるよ。
group_key: if you go around treating people poorly, it'll eventually bite you in the back.

source_en: First hold crime-prevention firmly in mind! Before going out, lock the doors!
target_ja: まずは防犯意識をしっかり持ちましょう！ 出かける前には戸締りを！
group_key: first hold crime-prevention firmly in mind! before going out, lock the doors!

source_en: I just moved.
target_ja: 私は引っ越してきたばかりです。
group_key: i just moved.

source_en: The two ships went down at once.
target_ja: 二隻の船が同時に沈んだ。
group_key: the two ships went down at once.

source_en: Where is the bus stop?
target_ja: バスの停留所はどこですか。
group_key: where is the bus stop?

source_en: I think twice before I smoke.
target_ja: 私は吸う前に吸って良いのか良く考える。
group_key: i think twice before i smoke.

source_en: Tom decided to take matters into his own hands.
target_ja: トムは自分で何とかすることに決めた。
group_key: tom decided to take matters into his own hands.

source_en: Tom put salt in his coffee instead of sugar.
target_ja: トムは砂糖の代わりに塩をコーヒーに入れてしまった。
group_key: tom put salt in his coffee instead of sugar.

source_en: He did the work on his own.
target_ja: 彼は独力でその仕事をした。
group_key: he did the work on his own.

source_en: I cannot hold the horse.
target_ja: この馬は手に負えない。
group_key: i cannot hold the horse.

source_en: Nobody can ever help me.
target_ja: 誰も決して私を助けることは出来ない。
group_key: nobody can ever help me.

source_en: My father has no longer a responsible position.
target_ja: 僕の父はもう責任のある地位にはついていません。
group_key: my father has no longer a responsible position.

source_en: The plane landed at 6 o'clock to the minute.
target_ja: 飛行機は６時きっかりに着陸した。
group_key: the plane landed at 6 o'clock to the minute.

source_en: All she could do was watch him walk away.
target_ja: 彼女は彼が去っていくのを見るだけで精一杯だった。
group_key: all she could do was watch him walk away.

source_en: Tom should've known better than to kiss Mary in public.
target_ja: 公衆の面前でメアリーにキスするなんて、トムも軽はずみなことしたもんだ。
group_key: tom should've known better than to kiss mary in public.

source_en: Tom was the one who took these pictures.
target_ja: この写真を撮ったのはトムだった。
group_key: tom was the one who took these pictures.

source_en: How awful to reflect that what people say of us is true!
target_ja: 人々が我々について言っていることが真実であることを反省してみると粛然とせざるをえない。
group_key: how awful to reflect that what people say of us is true!

source_en: It occurred to me that he had done it on purpose.
target_ja: 彼はわざとそうしたのだとふと私は思った。
group_key: it occurred to me that he had done it on purpose.

source_en: I thought that college would be a more interesting place.
target_ja: 大学ってもっと楽しいところだと思ってた。
group_key: i thought that college would be a more interesting place.

source_en: He said that he must see the manager.
target_ja: 彼は支配人に会わなければならないと言った。
group_key: he said that he must see the manager.

source_en: I don't know what to say...
target_ja: 何と言ったらいいか・・・。
group_key: i don't know what to say...

source_en: I can play the guitar.
target_ja: 私はギターが弾けます。
group_key: i can play the guitar.

source_en: The singer fought his way through the crowd of fans.
target_ja: その歌手はファンの群がる中を苦心して進んだ。
group_key: the singer fought his way through the crowd of fans.

source_en: It is absurd to believe that young children can run faster than policemen.
target_ja: 幼い子供が警官より走るのが速いと信じるのは理屈に合わない。
group_key: it is absurd to believe that young children can run faster than policemen.

source_en: Gordon is interested in the Japanese way of life.
target_ja: ゴードンは日本の生活様式に興味を持っている。
group_key: gordon is interested in the japanese way of life.

source_en: Show me your tongue.
target_ja: 舌を見せてください。
group_key: show me your tongue.

source_en: I have no idea where I left my keys.
target_ja: どこにカギを置き忘れたのかさっぱり分からないんだ。
group_key: i have no idea where i left my keys.

source_en: You ought not to smoke so much.
target_ja: きみはそんなにタバコを吸うべきではない。
group_key: you ought not to smoke so much.

source_en: The company laid off twenty percent of its work force; therefore, expenses decreased.
target_ja: その会社は社員の２０％を一時解雇した。その結果、経費は減少した。
group_key: the company laid off twenty percent of its work force; therefore, expenses decreased.

source_en: Excuse me, I'm an outsider, but please allow me to say a few words.
target_ja: すみません、部外者ですけど一言言わせてください。
group_key: excuse me, i'm an outsider, but please allow me to say a few words.

source_en: Please come to the counter at least an hour before your flight.
target_ja: 最低１時間前にはカウンターへいらっしゃってください。
group_key: please come to the counter at least an hour before your flight.

source_en: We'll never reach London before dark.
target_ja: 私たちは日暮れまでには絶対ロンドンに着かないだろう。
group_key: we'll never reach london before dark.

source_en: I'm very short.
target_ja: 私はとても背が低い。
group_key: i'm very short.

source_en: I want to get cigarettes.
target_ja: タバコを買いたいんです。
group_key: i want to get cigarettes.

source_en: Did you forget to talk to Tom this morning?
target_ja: 今朝、トムに言い忘れちゃったの？
group_key: did you forget to talk to tom this morning?

source_en: I like that movie too.
target_ja: その映画も好きよ。
group_key: i like that movie too.

source_en: I had to leave out this problem for lack of space.
target_ja: 紙面が足りなくてこの問題を省かなければならなかった。
group_key: i had to leave out this problem for lack of space.

source_en: The two fixed on the day for their wedding.
target_ja: 二人は結婚式の日を決めた。
group_key: the two fixed on the day for their wedding.

source_en: Women want to have sex too.
target_ja: 女性だってエッチしたい。
group_key: women want to have sex too.

source_en: My whole body feels heavy.
target_ja: 体全体がだるいです。
group_key: my whole body feels heavy.

source_en: I'll do the rest.
target_ja: 残りは僕がやるよ。
group_key: i'll do the rest.

source_en: I feel a lot better now, but I know Dad's going to be real upset.
target_ja: オラ気分が良くなったで。でもよ、おとうさんに滅茶苦茶怒られちまうと思うべさ。
group_key: i feel a lot better now, but i know dad's going to be real upset.

source_en: My little brother never helps around the house.
target_ja: 弟が家のお手伝いしないのよ。
group_key: my little brother never helps around the house.

source_en: Forget it!
target_ja: 忘れちまえ！
group_key: forget it!

source_en: The exhibition is now open.
target_ja: 展覧会は現在開催中です。
group_key: the exhibition is now open.

source_en: I'm not watching the TV.
target_ja: テレビは見てないけど。
group_key: i'm not watching the tv.

source_en: Does anyone know the Japanese language?
target_ja: 日本語ができる人はいますか？
group_key: does anyone know the japanese language?

source_en: The problem is Mr. Bark, the man whose secretary is away.
target_ja: 問題なのはバークさんなのよ。彼の秘書が今いないのよ。
group_key: the problem is mr. bark, the man whose secretary is away.

source_en: You may choose what to eat from this list.
target_ja: この表から食べたいものを選べます。
group_key: you may choose what to eat from this list.

source_en: I am afraid it is a reject.
target_ja: 不良品ではないかと思います。
group_key: i am afraid it is a reject.

source_en: She pulled herself together and started to talk again.
target_ja: 彼女は気を取り直し、また話し始めた。
group_key: she pulled herself together and started to talk again.

source_en: They gave him a chance to escape.
target_ja: 彼等は彼に逃げるきっかけを与えた。
group_key: they gave him a chance to escape.

source_en: Tom is boiling eggs.
target_ja: トムは卵を煮ている。
group_key: tom is boiling eggs.

source_en: You can still see the remains of the fortress there.
target_ja: そこでまだ要塞都市の遺跡をみることができる。
group_key: you can still see the remains of the fortress there.

source_en: Look out that you don't catch cold.
target_ja: 風邪をひかないように注意しなさい。
group_key: look out that you don't catch cold.

source_en: She had a happy childhood.
target_ja: 彼女は幼い頃幸せだった。
group_key: she had a happy childhood.

source_en: She is very exact in her job.
target_ja: 彼女は仕事が非常にきちょうめんだ。
group_key: she is very exact in her job.

source_en: Do you really think Tom is a spy?
target_ja: 本当にトムがスパイだと思ってるの？
group_key: do you really think tom is a spy?

source_en: In his autobiography, he repeatedly refers to his unhappy school days.
target_ja: 自伝の中で彼はくりかえし不幸な少年時代に言及している。
group_key: in his autobiography, he repeatedly refers to his unhappy school days.

source_en: He is a man of few words, but he always keeps his promise.
target_ja: 彼は言葉少ないが、必ず約束を守る人です。
group_key: he is a man of few words, but he always keeps his promise.

source_en: The picture is done in a masterly style.
target_ja: この絵は垢抜けしたかき方だ。
group_key: the picture is done in a masterly style.

source_en: I've lost my bag.
target_ja: 鞄をなくしてしまった。
group_key: i've lost my bag.

source_en: I had been studying mathematics about an hour, when I felt sleepy.
target_ja: 数学を１時間ほど勉強していたら、眠くなった。
group_key: i had been studying mathematics about an hour, when i felt sleepy.

source_en: You transfer at Ikebukuro Station.
target_ja: 池袋駅で乗り換えて下さい。
group_key: you transfer at ikebukuro station.

source_en: Looks like I might have to burn the midnight oil tonight.
target_ja: なんだか今夜は遅くまで残業になりそうだわ。
group_key: looks like i might have to burn the midnight oil tonight.

source_en: We're all going to share it. Could you bring us some extra plates?
target_ja: みんなで分けて食べたいので、取り皿をもらえますか。
group_key: we're all going to share it. could you bring us some extra plates?

source_en: You are far from a careful driver.
target_ja: あなたはけっして注意深いドライバーではありませんよ。
group_key: you are far from a careful driver.

source_en: This product is an intelligent integrated information system.
target_ja: このプロダクトはインテリジェント総合情報システムです。
group_key: this product is an intelligent integrated information system.

source_en: I made her acquaintance through his introduction.
target_ja: 彼の紹介で彼女と知り合った。
group_key: i made her acquaintance through his introduction.

source_en: Something is wrong with the engine.
target_ja: エンジンに何か故障がある。
group_key: something is wrong with the engine.

source_en: Do what you want.
target_ja: 勝手にしろ。
group_key: do what you want.

source_en: Do you remember seeing me before?
target_ja: 前に私に会ったこと覚えてる？
group_key: do you remember seeing me before?

source_en: Cars today are amazing.
target_ja: 最近の車はすごい。
group_key: cars today are amazing.

source_en: I prefer coffee to tea.
target_ja: 紅茶よりコーヒーの方が好きだ。
group_key: i prefer coffee to tea.

source_en: We stayed for 3 nights at a lakeside hotel.
target_ja: 湖畔のペンションに３泊しました。
group_key: we stayed for 3 nights at a lakeside hotel.

source_en: Esperanto grammar is very simple.
target_ja: エスペラントの文法は非常に簡単です。
group_key: esperanto grammar is very simple.

source_en: I am staying for another few weeks.
target_ja: あと数週間は滞在するつもりだよ。
group_key: i am staying for another few weeks.

source_en: He then added, "I tell you the truth..."
target_ja: そして言われた。「まことに、まことに、あなた方に告げます」
group_key: he then added, "i tell you the truth..."

source_en: He predicted there would be rain.
target_ja: 彼は雨になるだろうと予測した。
group_key: he predicted there would be rain.

source_en: Ouch! That hurts!
target_ja: 痛い！
group_key: ouch! that hurts!

source_en: How do you operate this machine?
target_ja: この機械はどうやって操作するのですか。
group_key: how do you operate this machine?

source_en: My father doesn't drink hard liquor.
target_ja: 父は強いお酒を飲みません。
group_key: my father doesn't drink hard liquor.

source_en: Tom's life is in danger.
target_ja: トムの命が危険にさらされている。
group_key: tom's life is in danger.

source_en: I am always ready to help you.
target_ja: 私はいつでも喜んでお手伝いします。
group_key: i am always ready to help you.

source_en: The birds flew towards a high tower.
target_ja: 鳥たちは、高い塔に向かって飛んで行きました。
group_key: the birds flew towards a high tower.

source_en: I understand.
target_ja: 分かった！
group_key: i understand.

source_en: It rained all afternoon.
target_ja: 午後はずっと雨だった。
group_key: it rained all afternoon.

source_en: You are easy to get on-board with flattery.
target_ja: すぐおだてに乗るんだから。
group_key: you are easy to get on-board with flattery.

source_en: The hearing were made off-limits to the press.
target_ja: 聴聞会は報道関係者を閉め出して行われました。
group_key: the hearing were made off-limits to the press.

source_en: Mary went to play with Tom.
target_ja: マリーはトムと遊びに行った。
group_key: mary went to play with tom.

source_en: My dream is to study French in Montreal.
target_ja: 夢は、モントリオールにフランス語留学することです。
group_key: my dream is to study french in montreal.

source_en: This bus is going to Boston.
target_ja: このバスはボストンに行きます。
group_key: this bus is going to boston.

source_en: Don't return evil for good.
target_ja: 恩をあだで返すな。
group_key: don't return evil for good.

source_en: Get out of there now.
target_ja: すぐにそこから出なさい。
group_key: get out of there now.

source_en: My children make pathetically sincere efforts to get up early.
target_ja: うちの子供達は早く起きるようにと涙ぐましい努力をしている。
group_key: my children make pathetically sincere efforts to get up early.

source_en: This grocery store only sells organic food.
target_ja: この食料品店は自然食品のみを売っている。
group_key: this grocery store only sells organic food.

source_en: I rented a boat by the hour.
target_ja: 私は時間ぎめでボートを借りた。
group_key: i rented a boat by the hour.
